item.title: Upskilling 50 SDETs with AI-Powered Test Automation
item.description:
DOMAIN: Gaming TYPE: Classroom Training DURATION: 5 Weeks SCALE: 50 SDETs

The Challenge

A fast-growing gaming organisation had 50 testers performing largely manual testing. As product complexity scaled and release cadence accelerated, the team needed to shift quickly toward automation — but lacked the skills, tooling fluency, and confidence to make that transition without structured support.

The goal: upskill all 50 SDETs to actively contribute to the existing test automation platform using modern tools and AI assistance — in five weeks, without pausing delivery.

Approach

Customised Curriculum

The programme was designed around the team’s existing tech stack, real test cases from their platform, and the specific gaps surfaced during a pre-training assessment. Nothing was generic — every module was contextualised to what the engineers would face on day one back at their desks.

AI-Assisted Automation Throughout

AI tools were woven into the curriculum from the start — not as a separate topic, but as a practical accelerant for writing, reviewing, and debugging test code. Engineers learned to use AI to move faster while building genuine understanding.

Daily Evaluations & Final Projects

Each day ended with a short evaluation to reinforce learning and catch gaps early. The final week required each participant to complete a hands-on project against the live automation platform — demonstrating real, integrated capability, not just classroom knowledge.

Technical Mentoring

Instructors provided continuous technical feedback throughout — reviewing code, unblocking individuals, and raising the floor across the cohort so no one was left behind.

Outcomes

   
Role transition 50 testers moved from manual to automation-ready contributors
Platform contribution All participants able to write, review, and extend existing test scripts
AI fluency Practical confidence using AI tools for automation tasks
Leadership identified Multiple participants demonstrated standout potential and were flagged for senior automation roles
Confidence Teams returned to delivery with measurably higher automation confidence and velocity

The measure of a training programme isn’t what people know at the end — it’s what they can do the week after. This cohort came back and shipped.

Discuss a training programme →   ← All Case Studies

item.title: Reliance Jio: Transforming Quality Engineering at Scale
item.description:
DOMAIN: Telecom · Retail · eComm · Media TYPE: Assessment · Strategy · Implementation DURATION: 4.5 Years SCALE: 20+ Teams · 75+ SDETs

The Challenge

Reliance Jio operates at massive scale — spanning telecom, retail, e-commerce, and media products across millions of users. As delivery velocity increased and product complexity grew, quality engineering needed to evolve from reactive testing to a structured, scalable capability embedded across 20+ product teams.

What Was Delivered

Assessment & Strategy

Reviewed existing practices across teams, identified bottlenecks and redundancies, and created an actionable quality roadmap aligned with business goals. The assessment surfaced the gap between perceived and actual quality — giving leadership a clear, evidence-based starting point.

Automation Frameworks

Built reusable test automation frameworks covering:

  • Web — cross-browser automation with stable, maintainable architecture
  • Mobile — Android and iOS native app testing (teswiz-based)
  • Desktop — Windows application automation
  • APIs — contract and integration testing

All frameworks were integrated with Azure DevOps quality gates — shifting quality left into the CI/CD pipeline and providing fast, actionable feedback on every build.

AI Visual Testing

Implemented AI-driven visual testing across applicable product surfaces — improving the reliability of visual validations at scale and reducing false positives that had been slowing release confidence.

QECC Establishment

Stood up a Quality Engineering Competency Centre (QECC):

  • Standardised tools, frameworks, and processes across teams
  • Sourced, recruited, and onboarded 75+ SDETs
  • Built competency maps and integrated engineers into active product teams
  • Created career pathways and mentoring structures to sustain the capability long-term

Outcomes

   
Coverage Automation across web, mobile, desktop, and API layers
CI/CD Integration Quality gates embedded in Azure DevOps pipelines for 20+ teams
Team Scale 75+ SDETs hired, onboarded, and integrated
Reliability Reduced visual regression false positives via AI-driven testing
Sustainability Competency centre with standardised frameworks and clear career structure

A long-term engagement that moved quality from a team-level concern to an organisational capability — embedded in processes, tooling, and people.

Discuss a similar engagement →   ← All Case Studies

item.title: WAAT
item.description:
• WAAT - Web Analytics Automated Testing Framework
  • WAAT-Ruby - Web Analytics Automated Testing Framework (Ruby)

item.title: Transformation & Advisory
item.description:
Quality transformation is not a one-size-fits-all exercise. Organisations differ in maturity, culture, team structure, and delivery context. I work with leadership and engineering teams to assess where they are, define where they need to be, and build a practical path to get there — whether that means a focused advisory engagement, a full transformation programme, or targeted training and upskilling for the team doing the work.

WHAT THIS LOOKS LIKE

In Practice

◐

Current-State Assessment

A structured review of your quality practices, tooling, team capability, and delivery pipeline. Surfaces the gap between perceived and actual quality — and identifies the highest-leverage areas for improvement.

■

Quality Operating Model Design

Define governance frameworks, ownership structures, and risk-based practices that align quality outcomes with business goals and delivery cadence. Build shared accountability across product, engineering, and QA.

▲

Transformation Roadmap

A pragmatic, prioritised plan to move from current state to target state — phased to deliver early wins while building towards sustainable capability. Contextual to your product, team, and technology.

◆

Team Structure & Capability Building

Advice on team composition, role definitions, and career pathways for QA and SDET functions. Identifies skill gaps and defines the upskilling path — from manual testers to automation-ready contributors, and from individual contributors to leads.

★

Training & Upskilling

Hands-on training programmes to build the skills the team needs to execute the transformation — covering automation fundamentals, AI-assisted testing, quality strategy for managers, and more. Delivered as structured bootcamps, workshops, or embedded coaching. View all training programmes →

●

Stakeholder Alignment

Facilitated workshops and executive briefings to build shared understanding of quality risk, delivery confidence, and the value of investment in quality engineering capability.

►

Contextual Implementation Support

Hands-on involvement in rolling out the roadmap — from embedding quality gates in CI/CD to coaching teams, reviewing frameworks, and tracking progress against defined quality signals.

ENGAGEMENT MODELS

How We Work Together

Advisory Engagement

Short-term, focused engagements — typically a few days to a few weeks. Ideal for organisations that need an expert perspective on a specific challenge: a quality assessment, a test strategy review, a roadmap workshop, or a second opinion before a major delivery decision.

Transformation Programme

Longer-term engagements that span weeks to months. Covers the full arc from assessment through roadmap design to implementation support. Suited to organisations undergoing significant change — scaling engineering teams, adopting AI-assisted delivery, or rebuilding quality capability after a period of technical debt accumulation.

Training & Upskilling Programme

Structured training delivered to individuals or teams — as standalone programmes or embedded within a broader transformation. Formats include bootcamps, classroom sessions, online workshops, and ongoing coaching. Topics range from automation fundamentals and AI-assisted testing to quality strategy for managers and SDET capability building. Programmes are tailored to your team's existing skills, tools, and delivery context.

See all training programmes →

TRACK RECORD

Domains & Experience

I have led quality transformations and upskilling programmes across a wide range of industries and organisational contexts:

  • Telecom — large-scale SDLC transformation with distributed QA teams; quality operating model redesign
  • E-commerce — automation strategy, test coverage improvement, and team upskilling at speed
  • Gaming — upskilled 50 SDETs through a 5-week AI-powered automation bootcamp; transitioned manual testers to automation-ready roles
  • SaaS — quality operating model design, CI/CD quality gate integration, and manager-level quality strategy workshops
  • Banking & Financial Services — risk-based test strategy and compliance-aware quality frameworks
  • Enterprise Platforms — cross-functional quality ownership, defect leakage reduction, and SDET capability building

Engagements have ranged from targeted advisory work with leadership teams to full embedded transformation programmes and large-scale upskilling initiatives spanning multiple quarters.

Have a quality challenge you'd like to talk through?

Get in touch →
I have successfully driven quality transformations across telecom, e-commerce, gaming, SaaS, banking/financial services, and enterprise platforms — delivering scalable frameworks, reduced defect leakage, and stronger QA and SDET teams.

item.title: Trainings and Workshops
item.description:
Practical, hands-on programmes designed to upskill individuals and teams — from manual testers to automation-ready contributors. All programmes can be tailored to your team's specific requirements, existing challenges, or technology stack.

PROGRAMMES

Training Programmes

01

Quality Strategy in the AI-Accelerated World

Half-day or full-day workshop for managers: building product confidence when delivery is faster, more distributed, and AI-supported. Covers quality risk identification, delivery bottleneck visualisation, quality signals, and co-creating a high-level quality strategy — using a real-world case study.

View full workshop details →

02

Boosting Automation with AI

Apply AI practically to make automation smarter, faster, deterministic and resilient.

03

Future-Ready Automation Frameworks

Design scalable, maintainable frameworks that grow with your product and teams.

04

Quality Metrics in the Age of AI

Track what truly matters — modern metrics for intelligent, data-driven quality decisions.

05

Test Automation Coverage & ROI

Measure what your automation truly covers and the business value it delivers.

06

Automation Framework 101

Learn the fundamentals of building robust automation for web and mobile applications.

07

Visual AI for Test Coverage

Expand and accelerate UI test coverage for web and mobile using Visual AI tools.

+

Custom Programme

Workshops and training programmes tailored to your team's specific requirements, challenges, or technology stack.

CASE STUDY

Upskilling 50 SDETs with AI-Powered Test Automation

🎮 Gaming 🏫 Classroom Training ⏱ 5 Weeks 👥 50 SDETs
  • Upskilled 50 SDETs to leverage AI tools and modern automation technologies
  • Rigorous classroom-based programme with customised curriculum, AI-assisted automation, daily evaluations, and final projects
  • Transitioned testers from manual to automation-ready roles; multiple participants demonstrated leadership potential in automation

Interested in a training programme for your team?

Get in touch →

item.title: Test Automation
item.description:
Starting a test automation framework is straightforward. Keeping it scalable, maintainable, and reliable as your product and team grow — that is where most organisations struggle.

I leverage AI and industry best practices to design automation solutions that are fast, deterministic, and built to last — across web, mobile (Android & iOS), APIs, desktop, and visual testing.

What I help teams build

AI-Driven Automation Strategy — Apply AI practically to make automation smarter, faster, and more resilient. Reduce flakiness, accelerate coverage, and improve feedback cycle times across CI/CD pipelines.

Future-Ready Automation Frameworks — Design scalable, maintainable frameworks that grow with your product and team. Based on your context, I identify the right automation pyramid and help you build it right from the start.

Visual AI Testing — Expand and accelerate UI test coverage for web and mobile using Visual AI tools — catching visual regressions that traditional assertions miss.

Test Automation Coverage & ROI — Measure what your automation truly covers and the business value it delivers. Move beyond vanity metrics to data-informed quality decisions.

CI/CD Native Integration — Automation built for real-world pipeline integration — not just local runs. Fast feedback, reliable gates, and actionable reporting at every stage.

My open-source framework teswiz is used globally and supports web, mobile, API, and desktop automation out of the box — with CI/CD integration built in.
